name: Manhattan Clam Chowder
recipeIngredient: 3 cans (6 ½ oz.) minced clams, drain the juice
16 ounces undrained tomatoes, cut into chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, cook bacon until crisp. Remove and drain the bacon, reserving 2 tablespoons drippings in the saucepan.
2. Crumble the cooked bacon and set aside.
3. Sauté onions, celery, and green pepper in the reserved drippings until tender.
4. Add water, potatoes, carrots, parsley, salt, thyme, pepper, and juice from clams and tomatoes to the pan.
5. Bring mixture to a boil. Reduce heat and cover; simmer for 25-35 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
6. Remove bay leaf. Stir in minced clams.
7. Heat gently, stirring frequently, but do not boil.
8. Garnish each serving with crumbled bacon.
Yield: 6 servings (1 ½ cups each)
